zh.Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭培爾盧維 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Sinclair 4th Earl Of Strathearn於大約1176年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾盧維時，他的父親Sir Gille Brigte Third Earl of Strathearn27歲，母親Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn35歲。他和Matilda de Moravia至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1244年8月在蘇格蘭培爾盧維享年69歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭培爾盧維。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Sinclair 4th Earl Of Strathearn was born about 1176, in Ruthven, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Gille Brigte Third Earl of Strathearn, was 27 and his mother, Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn, was 35. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Matilda de Moravia. He died in August 1244, in Ruthven, Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 69, and was buried in Ruthven, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Sinclair 4th Earl Of Strathearn est né environ 1176, à Ruthven, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Gille Brigte Third Earl of Strathearn, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn, avait 35 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Matilda de Moravia. Il est décédé en août 1244, à Ruthven, Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Ruthven, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Scotland: Ruthven Castle, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert Sinclair 4th Earl Of Strathearn um 1176 in Ruthven, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Gille Brigte Third Earl of Strathearn 27 und seine Mutter Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn 35. Er hatte mit Matilda de Moravia mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb im August 1244 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Ruthven, Perthshire, Scotland und wurde in Ruthven, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Sinclair 4th Earl Of Strathearnが約1176年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアルスベンで生まれた時，父Sir Gille Brigte Third Earl of Strathearnは27歳，母Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearnは35歳であった。彼はMatilda de Moraviaとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1244年8月にスコットランドペルスシュアイアルスベンで69歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアルスベンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Sinclair 4th Earl Of Strathearn(이)가 대략 1176년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 루스븐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Gille Brigte Third Earl of Strathearn(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn(은)는 35세였다. 그는 Matilda de Moravia(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1244년 8월 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 루스븐에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 루스븐에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Sinclair 4th Earl Of Strathearn родился около 1176 г. в местности рутвен, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Gille Brigte Third Earl of Strathearn было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn, 35. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Matilda de Moravia. Он умер август 1244 г. в местности рутвен, пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности рутвен, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
